{
  "judge_id": "Judge-123",
  "active_set_recognized": ["R1.1", "R1.2", "R1.4", "RX.1", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "scores": {
    "R1.1": 4,
    "R1.2": 4,
    "R1.4": 3,
    "RX.1": 4,
    "RX.3": 5,
    "RX.4": 5,
    "RX.5": 4
  },
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "The agent identifies several conceptual issues, including overclaimed generality and internal inconsistencies, and proposes reframing to address these issues.",
    "R1.2": "The agent walks the argument chain end-to-end, flags every load-bearing inconsistency, and proposes minimal repairs.",
    "R1.4": "The agent names one prior work (safe RL paradigm) but does not articulate the delta clearly; more citations are needed to support the novelty claim.",
    "RX.1": "The agent flags uncertainty for several claims, including the specific commitment percentages and the existence of ablations on the three mechanisms.",
    "RX.3": "The output covers exactly the agreed active set J*(d).",
    "RX.4": "No regressions; identifies harmful patterns in the original text, such as overclaimed generality and internal inconsistencies.",
    "RX.5": "Every non-cosmetic change is annotated with source and reason, including specific page numbers or section references."
  },
  "confidence": {
    "R1.1": 0.9,
    "R1.2": 0.8,
    "R1.4": 0.7,
    "RX.1": 0.8,
    "RX.3": 1.0,
    "RX.4": 0.9,
    "RX.5": 0.8
  },
  "blockers": []
}

rationale.R1.1: The agent identifies several conceptual issues, including overclaimed generality and internal inconsistencies, and proposes reframing to address these issues.
rationale.R1.2: The agent walks the argument chain end-to-end, flags every load-bearing inconsistency, and proposes minimal repairs.
rationale.R1.4: The agent names one prior work (safe RL paradigm) but does not articulate the delta clearly; more citations are needed to support the novelty claim.
rationale.RX.1: The agent flags uncertainty for several claims, including the specific commitment percentages and the existence of ablations on the three mechanisms.
rationale.RX.3: The output covers exactly the agreed active set J*(d).
rationale.RX.4: No regressions; identifies harmful patterns in the original text, such as overclaimed generality and internal inconsistencies.
rationale.RX.5: Every non-cosmetic change is annotated with source and reason, including specific page numbers or section references.
